action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 13 crashed after 17 seconds in DDP when trying to freeze the DINOv2 backbone. The pattern (rank 0 exits immediately, others get SIGTERM) suggests DDP incompatibility with frozen parameters or a setup bug. Analysis: DDP has been consistently problematic in this environment, while single-GPU training is much simpler and stable. Given frozen-backbone training (only ~265K head params trainable), a single GPU with batch size 256 is efficient enough (~10 min/epoch). The 15-epoch full fine-tuning got 2.31% (worse than 2-epoch's 4.71%), confirming catastrophic forgetting. Strategy: freeze backbone entirely, train only the classification head for 10 epochs with high LR (1e-2), domain-balanced sampling, and strong augmentations. This preserves SSL features while learning a domain-robust head. Decision: Write a robust single-GPU train.py, dataset.py, keep model.py and predict.py compatible. Run 10 epochs and submit eval immediately after training.
